content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

November 19, 2007

Wade Arnold
506 Plumas #24
Yuba City, CA 95991

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-07-171

Dear Mr. Arnold:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please note that we base our advice solely on the provisions of the Act. We therefore offer no opinion on the application, if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict of interest or Government Code Section 1099 dealing with incompatible offices. We recommend that you seek appropriate counsel with respect to these provisions.

As we discussed in our telephone conversation on November 16, 2007, the facts you presented do not involve a question on which we can offer advice. Accordingly, no response to your letter is necessary at this time and we have withdrawn your request for advice. The Commission does not offer advice if a request does not pose a question under the Act, or if the request is hypothetical. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D), (c)(4)(D).) Should the occasion arise where you have a decision before you that could pose a potential conflict of interest, please do not hesitate to contact us for advice.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

sections.conclusion: The facts presented do not involve a question on which the Commission can offer advice. Accordingly, the request for advice has been withdrawn. The Commission does not offer advice if a request does not pose a question under the Act, or if the request is hypothetical.
sections.question_synthetic: Wade Arnold sought advice regarding potential conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, but after discussion with FPPC staff, the request was determined not to pose an actual question under the Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the advice request because the facts presented did not constitute an actual question under the Political Reform Act, as the Commission cannot advise on hypothetical matters or non-questions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter with no distinct QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. Question and conclusion synthesized from narrative text. Document includes disclaimer that FPPC opinion is limited to Political Reform Act provisions only.

CONCLUSION: The facts presented do not involve a question on which the Commission can offer advice. Accordingly, the request for advice has been withdrawn. The Commission does not offer advice if a request does not pose a question under the Act, or if the request is hypothetical.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdrew Wade Arnold's request for advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions because the facts presented did not pose an actual question under the Political Reform Act. The letter recommends that Arnold contact the Commission if a future decision presents a potential conflict of interest.
